Rheinmetall demonstrates ramjet-assisted 155mm projectile reaching 70 km

Shephard News Team · 24 June 2026

Rheinmetall has demonstrated a ramjet-assisted 155mm artillery projectile that reached a range of approximately 70 km in recent trials, a significant step in extended-range artillery ammunition. The test was conducted jointly with partners and marks progress toward a deployable round.

The ramjet projectile is part of a broader effort to extend artillery reach well beyond current ERFB and base-bleed rounds. Maturity remains at the test stage, with fielding expected around 2027-2028.

Analysts said the technology could reshape counter-battery and deep-fire doctrine if it reaches production.
